note: Explorer address dashboard: per-row click-to-expand explanations. Every balance row (TOTAL BALANCE, MINED, RECEIVED currently held, GROSS received lifetime, GROSS sent lifetime, NET external outflow, DTD LOTTERY current, DTD LOTTERY lifetime, SPENDABLE, IMMATURE) is now clickable. The label shows a small chevron; clicking it toggles a dropdown body that spans both grid columns and reveals a plain-English explanation: what the metric measures, snapshot vs lifetime semantics, gross vs net, what zero means, and the relationship to neighboring rows. Multiple rows can be open at once; state is purely DOM-local. Implementation: new _addrBalRow(id, labelHtml, valueHtml, helpHtml, labelStyle, extraLblAttrs) helper that emits the three grid children (k.k-help + v + k-help-body); new _toggleAddrHelp(id) flips both .open classes. Existing async-hydration ids (addrRecvGrossLbl, addrSentLbl, addrNetExtLbl, addrRecvGrossVal, addrSentVal, addrNetExtVal) preserved so getaddressflows still finds the value cells. Existing tooltip text (title attr) is replaced by the richer expandable body for the 9 refactored rows. CSS uses max-height transition for smooth open/close. NO consensus / RPC / node / miner / wallet / wire-format change. NO new endpoints.
